title: Add long-horizon watcher prompt template and bug-spinout skill
description: Externalize watcher execution prompt into a reusable long-horizon template and add a formal project skill that defines complex-blocker bug spinout workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher supports prompt template injection with issue/root placeholders, long-horizon prompt file is versioned, bug-spinout skill exists with deterministic command sequence, and docs mention both.
notes: Implemented reusable prompts/long-horizon-bead-work.md template with issue/root placeholders, wired scripts/bead_watcher.sh to PROMPT_TEMPLATE, added skills/bug-blocker-spinout/SKILL.md workflow, and documented both in README.md and AGENTS.md.

title: Human escalation: osm-4x3.1 exceeded watcher retries
description: Watcher reached 3 retries for osm-4x3.1 without closure/block transition. Human decision required.
notes: Created while validating watcher runner before model/default compatibility fix. Closed after remediation; no human action required.

acceptance_criteria: All child issues are closed with passing evidence attached in notes, and the epic is only closed after integration validation confirms no open blockers remain.

title: Unblock watcher writes in codex exec sandbox
description: Long-horizon watcher currently invokes codex exec in a read-only sandbox despite requested workspace-write. Codex then cannot run bd/git writes and bead churn stalls.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher codex runs can execute bd update/close/sync and git operations, one-cycle smoke test changes bead state deterministically, and no permission errors appear in console logs.
notes: Cancelled: watcher-based automation was intentionally removed in favor of manual live-tab execution.

title: Fix bead graph blockers and rollout gates
description: Apply audit fixes: add missing cross-epic test deps, add rollout stage gates, keep epics out of ready queue, and align issue prefix with repo name.
acceptance_criteria: All identified graph fixes are applied: cross-epic dependencies verified, rollout stage chain exists with ordered blockers, acceptance criteria backfilled on non-closed issues, and prefix migration completed or explicitly documented.

title: Fix watcher live-run failures (model default and nounset map)
description: Live tmux run exposed unsupported model default and nounset crashes in retry bookkeeping. Fix and clean accidental escalation artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher defaults to supported model, retry bookkeeping is stable under /bin/bash with nounset, and accidental escalation bead artifacts are cleared/closed with notes.
notes: Changed MODEL default to gpt-5, replaced retry map with string-based key/value state compatible with /bin/bash + nounset, and cleaned erroneous escalation beads (osm-0ny, osm-6zx) plus blocked dependencies.

title: Implement WP-1 and WP-2 core watcher runtime
description: Build core FSM watcher loop, lease lock/heartbeat, idempotency store, and stale-run recovery per SPEC.orchestrator-handoff.md sections 22.1-22.2.
acceptance_criteria: All child issues are closed with passing evidence attached in notes, and the epic is only closed after integration validation confirms no open blockers remain.
notes: Tracking epic only. Execute and close child tasks before reopening/closing this epic.

title: Define watcher module boundaries and interfaces
description: Create concrete module/file boundaries, public interfaces, and lifecycle contracts for the watcher engine, lock manager, and state store.
acceptance_criteria: Contract artifact is committed with field-level semantics and invariants, invalid-input behavior is specified, and at least one validation test confirms parser/schema enforcement.
notes: Implemented watcher module/interface contract and strict handoff schema artifacts at contracts/watcher-module-contract.md and contracts/handoff.schema.json. Added validator script scripts/validate_handoff_schema.sh and executable test tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh with fixtures under tests/fixtures/. Validation run: ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh (PASS).

title: Add runtime integration tests for success and failure paths
description: Validate end-to-end transitions for happy path, retriable failure, non-retriable failure, and stale recovery.
acceptance_criteria: New tests reproduce the target scenarios from the issue description, fail when behavior is broken, pass with current implementation, and run in the documented local test command.

title: Implement poll loop and candidate selection
description: Implement watcher loop with configurable poll interval and selection of eligible QUEUED handoffs.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented poll and candidate selection module at src/watcher/poll_loop.py with configurable interval, deterministic sorting (priority, updated_at, id), limit handling, and parser-backed eligibility checks for QUEUED handoffs. Added tests in tests/test_poll_loop.py covering primary selection path, deterministic filtering/order, limit edge case, and nonpositive interval failure. Validation run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py ; ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh (PASS).

title: Implement handoff parser and schema validator
description: Parse handoff notes block and enforce schema constraints from spec section 4.4 before any execution.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented parser module src/watcher/handoff_parser.py to extract handoff block content from notes and enforce section 4.4 schema constraints, including dotted bead IDs for origin_id and bug_id. Added tests in tests/test_handoff_parser.py for success, missing-block failure, and invalid-field failure paths. Validation commands run and passing: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_handoff_parser.py ; ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh.

title: Implement FSM transition executor
description: Implement deterministic transitions for QUEUED, RUNNING, RETRY_WAIT, DONE, and HUMAN_REQUIRED states.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented deterministic FSM transition executor in src/watcher/fsm.py for states QUEUED, RUNNING, RETRY_WAIT, DONE, and HUMAN_REQUIRED with explicit events and precondition checks. Covered success path, retriable failure path, non-retriable escalation path, cooldown re-entry, and invalid transition failures. Added tests in tests/test_fsm.py and re-ran full test set with python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py plus shell schema validation test (all PASS).

title: Implement label invariant validator and normalization
description: Validate label invariants and apply deterministic normalization/escalation for invalid combinations.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented label invariant validator and deterministic normalization/escalation logic in src/watcher/label_invariants.py. Enforced single primary state label, constrained needs:human coexistence, deterministic partial normalization cases, and escalation to needs:human + orchestrator:dead with error_class=fsm_invalid for ambiguous combinations. Added tests in tests/test_label_invariants.py for unchanged valid path, missing-primary normalization, partial-claim normalization, ambiguous multi-state escalation, and invalid needs:human coexistence escalation. Validation run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py ; ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh (PASS).

title: Implement lease lock and heartbeat lifecycle
description: Create lock acquisition, heartbeat renewal, stale lease detection, and ownership semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented lease lock and heartbeat lifecycle in src/watcher/lease_lock.py with acquisition, ownership checks, heartbeat renewal, stale lease detection, and controlled release semantics. Added deterministic tests in tests/test_lease_lock.py covering happy path, active-lock contention, stale reclaim, heartbeat renewal, ownership mismatch failure, and expired heartbeat failure. Validation run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py ; ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh (PASS).

title: Implement idempotency state store with atomic updates
description: Implement persistent handoff-key state store and atomic read-modify-write updates.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.
notes: Implemented idempotency state store with atomic updates in src/watcher/state_store.py at the spec path shape (.beads/orchestrator-handoff-state.json). Added StateRecord model, optimistic version checks, conflict signaling, and lock-guarded load/save/update_atomic operations with atomic file replacement writes. Added tests in tests/test_state_store.py for roundtrip persistence, expected-version conflict behavior, atomic update mutation, and initialization of missing keys. Validation run: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py ; ./tests/test_handoff_schema_validation.sh (PASS).

title: Implement stale RUNNING reconciler
description: Detect stale RUNNING attempts and transition them through retry or human-required paths per policy.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation matches the spec section in issue description, includes deterministic handling for success/failure paths, and tests cover primary behavior plus at least one edge/failure case.

title: Add concurrency and race-condition tests for runtime core
description: Add deterministic tests for dual watcher contention, lock ownership, and idempotent behavior.
acceptance_criteria: New tests reproduce the target scenarios from the issue description, fail when behavior is broken, pass with current implementation, and run in the documented local test command.

title: Add resume-session mode to bead watcher
description: Allow watcher loop to reuse the same Codex session via codex exec resume so tmux can keep activating a persistent conversation context.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher supports CODEX_SESSION_ID and CODEX_RESUME_LAST mode, docs include usage, and script still passes shell syntax/help checks.
notes: Implemented CODEX_SESSION_ID/CODEX_RESUME_LAST modes in scripts/bead_watcher.sh and documented usage in README.md; validated via bash -n and --help.

title: Human escalation: osm-4x3.1 exceeded watcher retries
description: Watcher reached 3 retries for osm-4x3.1 without closure/block transition. Human decision required.
notes: Created during watcher bootstrap failure (resume flag mismatch). Closed after watcher fix; no human action required.

title: Rollout gates: Stage 0-3
description: Operational rollout control chain from dry simulation to full automation with explicit go/no-go gates.
acceptance_criteria: All child issues are closed with passing evidence attached in notes, and the epic is only closed after integration validation confirms no open blockers remain.
notes: Tracking epic only. Execute stage gate children in order (Stage 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3); each gate is go/no-go and must be explicitly closed with evidence in notes.

title: Stage 0 gate: dry-run simulation signoff
description: Run watcher in simulation mode only. Require deterministic replay parity and zero side effects against production state.
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run mode shows deterministic replay parity on representative handoff samples, produces no production side effects, and approval notes include approver identity and timestamp.

title: Stage 1 gate: shadow mode signoff
description: Enable shadow execution with read-only policy eval and full observability capture. Human approves before assisted actions.
acceptance_criteria: Shadow mode runs continuously with complete observability artifacts, divergence alerts stay within agreed threshold, and human approver records go/no-go in gate notes.

title: Stage 2 gate: assisted mode signoff
description: Enable human-confirmed execution path with retry/dead-letter safety controls and digest/escalation visibility.
acceptance_criteria: Assisted mode requires explicit human confirmation for actions, retry/dead-letter safeguards are exercised in validation runs, and escalation digest delivery is verified end-to-end.

title: Stage 3 gate: full automation signoff
description: Enable full automation only after end-to-end reliability evidence and rollback drill success are documented.
acceptance_criteria: Full automation gate closes only after end-to-end reliability tests pass, rollback drill succeeds, and on-call runbook links are attached to gate notes.

title: Documentation polish for human and agent consumers
description: Improve top-level docs for two audiences: humans onboarding to the project and agents executing work safely and efficiently.
acceptance_criteria: README and AGENTS tasks are completed with cross-checked commands, no contradictory instructions remain, and onboarding/run workflow is clear to first-time contributors and coding agents.
notes: All child tasks complete: README and AGENTS docs improved and consistency pass executed.

title: Refactor README for fast human onboarding
description: Rewrite README.md into a concise human-first onboarding doc: project purpose, architecture snapshot, setup, daily workflow, and troubleshooting quick hits.
acceptance_criteria: README includes: purpose, prerequisites, setup commands, how to run core workflows, where specs/issues live, and a short troubleshooting section; commands are verified against current repo tooling.
notes: Completed by rewriting README.md with human-first onboarding, workflow commands, layout, reliability checks, and troubleshooting section.

title: Restructure AGENTS guidance for deterministic agent execution
description: Tighten AGENTS.md for agent use: clear operating modes, mandatory session-end steps, bead workflow, and failure/escalation behaviors with unambiguous command examples.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md clearly defines start-of-session triage, issue claiming, implementation flow, verification gates, sync/push requirements, and fallback behavior when tools fail; no ambiguous or conflicting steps remain.
notes: Completed by restructuring AGENTS.md with deterministic session start, working loop, escalation protocol, and explicit landing-the-plane checklist.

title: Cross-check README and AGENTS for consistency
description: Perform consistency sweep so README and AGENTS reference the same branch strategy, beads commands, and session-close checklist.
acceptance_criteria: All shared commands and workflow language are consistent between files; at least one dry-run onboarding pass validates docs end-to-end; differences are intentional and documented.
notes: Cross-checked README.md and AGENTS.md command flow and terminology; both now align on bd workflow and push requirements.

title: Add long-horizon swarm prompt and skill
description: Create reusable long-horizon prompt for live bead execution and add a skill that guides swarm-mode execution with bug spinout/switch protocol.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt file exists locally with clear loop/closure rules; skill exists with trigger metadata for swarm scenarios; protocol includes creating bug bead + blocking/switching on complex blockers; changes are pushed.
notes: Added reusable long-horizon swarm prompt at prompts/bead-swarm-long-horizon.md and added skill skills/bead-swarm-long-horizon/SKILL.md with trigger metadata for sustained bead-churn work. Skill includes mandatory complex-blocker bug spinout + parent-block + focus-switch protocol. README now points to the prompt for swarm execution.

title: Increase orchestration spec granularity
description: Review SPEC.orchestrator-handoff.md and add implementation-level detail: concrete work packages, data contracts, command interfaces, rollout/runbook, and phase exit criteria.
notes: Expanded plan into granular execution detail: WP-1..WP-7 work packages, concrete data contracts, control-flow scenarios, rollout gates, SLOs, on-call runbook, override policy, and disaster recovery procedures.

notes: Patched script to remove associative arrays for bash3 compatibility, fixed codex_rc capture logic, removed unsupported -o in resume mode, and cleaned accidental escalation bead osm-6zx plus dependency side effects.

description: Add tests for precedence order, conflict detection, and policy_ambiguous fail-closed behavior.
acceptance_criteria: New tests reproduce the target scenarios from the issue description, fail when behavior is broken, pass with current implementation, and run in the documented local test command.

title: Clarify bug_id schema regex versus dotted bead IDs
description: Current normative regex for bug_id (^ [a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{1,63} $) disallows dotted IDs, but existing bead IDs may contain dots (for child issues). Confirm intended contract and update schema/spec/tests accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Decision documented and schema/spec updated so parser behavior for dotted bug IDs is deterministic; parser task references the decision bead.
notes: Decision: dotted bead IDs are allowed for origin_id/bug_id. Updated normative regex in SPEC section 4.4, updated contracts/handoff.schema.json, updated scripts/validate_handoff_schema.sh, and updated tests to assert dotted valid IDs and explicit bug_id invalid detection.

title: Override watcher reasoning effort for gpt-5 compatibility
description: Codex config used model_reasoning_effort=xhigh which is invalid for gpt-5, causing watcher codex runs to fail.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher passes model_reasoning_effort override compatible with gpt-5 and docs expose override knob.
notes: Added MODEL_REASONING_EFFORT env var defaulting to high and injected -c model_reasoning_effort in codex exec and resume commands. README includes override example.

title: Add autonomous bead watcher runner with retry/escalation guards
description: Create a script that repeatedly picks ready beads, invokes codex exec to process one bead per cycle, stops when no ready beads remain, and enforces finite-state retry/loop protection.
acceptance_criteria: Script runs one-bead-at-a-time until queue is empty, has max retry + max cycle safeguards to prevent infinite loops, supports optional human escalation hook, and documents complex-bug spinout behavior (create bug bead, block parent, focus bug first).
notes: Implemented scripts/bead_watcher.sh with one-bead cycles, finite retry/no-progress guards, escalation bead creation, optional human notification hook, and complex-bug spinout instructions in codex prompt. Updated README.md and AGENTS.md with usage.
